answer:
Let $f(x) = \frac{(x-p)(x-q)}{x-r}$. We are given that $f(x) \le 0$ if and only if $x < -6$ or $|x-30| \le 2$.
This means that $f(x) \le 0$ if and only if $x < -6$ or $28 \le x \le 32$.
We have $x < -6$ or $28 \le x \le 32$.
We are given that $p < q$.
We want to find $p+2q+3r$.

Since $f(x) \le 0$ if and only if $x < -6$ or $28 \le x \le 32$, we can consider the intervals $(-\infty, -6)$ and $[28, 32]$.
We have $f(x) > 0$ if and only if $-6 < x < 28$.
We have $f(x) < 0$ if and only if $x \le -6$ or $x > 32$.

We are given that $f(x) \le 0$ if and only if $x < -6$ or $|x-30| \le 2$.
This means that $f(x) \le 0$ if and only if $x < -6$ or $28 \le x \le 32$.
Let's consider the case where $x = 30$. Then $f(30) = \frac{(30-p)(30-q)}{30-r} \le 0$.
Since $p < q$, we have $30-p > 30-q$.
If $30-r > 0$, then $(30-p)(30-q) \le 0$, so $p \ge 30$ or $q \le 30$.
If $30-r < 0$, then $(30-p)(30-q) \ge 0$, so $p \le 30$ and $q \ge 30$.
We are given that $x < -6$ or $|x-30| \le 2$.
If $x < -6$, then $x-r < -6-r$.
If $x = -6$, then $f(-6) = \frac{(-6-p)(-6-q)}{-6-r} = \frac{(6+p)(6+q)}{6+r} \le 0$.
If $x = 28$, then $f(28) = \frac{(28-p)(28-q)}{28-r} \le 0$.
If $x = 32$, then $f(32) = \frac{(32-p)(32-q)}{32-r} \le 0$.

Let $x = 30$. Then $|x-30| = 0 \le 2$, so $x=30$ is in the interval $[28, 32]$.
We have $f(30) = \frac{(30-p)(30-q)}{30-r} \le 0$.
If $p=29$ and $q=31$, then $f(30) = \frac{(30-29)(30-31)}{30-r} = \frac{(1)(-1)}{30-r} = \frac{-1}{30-r} \le 0$.
This implies $30-r < 0$, so $r > 30$.
Let $r=31$. Then $p+2q+3r = 29 + 2(31) + 3(31) = 29 + 62 + 93 = 184$.

Let $p=28$ and $q=32$. Then $f(x) = \frac{(x-28)(x-32)}{x-r}$.
We have $x < -6$ or $|x-30| \le 2$.
If $x < -6$, then $f(x) < 0$.
If $|x-30| \le 2$, then $-2 \le x-30 \le 2$, so $28 \le x \le 32$.
Then $f(x) = \frac{(x-28)(x-32)}{x-r} \le 0$.
If $r=30$, then $f(x) = \frac{(x-28)(x-32)}{x-30}$.
$f(28) = 0$, $f(32) = 0$.
If $x=29$, then $f(29) = \frac{(29-28)(29-32)}{29-30} = \frac{(1)(-3)}{-1} = 3 > 0$.
If $x=31$, then $f(31) = \frac{(31-28)(31-32)}{31-30} = \frac{(3)(-1)}{1} = -3 < 0$.
So $r=30$ is possible.
Then $p+2q+3r = 28 + 2(32) + 3(30) = 28 + 64 + 90 = 182$.

Consider $p=28, q=32, r=30$. Then $f(x) = \frac{(x-28)(x-32)}{x-30}$.
We have $f(x) \le 0$ if $x < -6$ or $28 \le x \le 32$.
If $x = -7$, then $f(-7) = \frac{(-7-28)(-7-32)}{-7-30} = \frac{(-35)(-39)}{-37} = \frac{1365}{-37} < 0$.
If $x = 29$, then $f(29) = \frac{(29-28)(29-32)}{29-30} = \frac{(1)(-3)}{-1} = 3 > 0$.
If $x = 31$, then $f(31) = \frac{(31-28)(31-32)}{31-30} = \frac{(3)(-1)}{1} = -3 < 0$.

Final Answer: The final answer is $\boxed{182}$
